## Context length benchmarks
### **Llama 3.3 (70B) max. context length**
We tested Llama 3.3 (70B) Instruct on a 80GB A100 and did 4bit QLoRA on all linear layers (Q, K, V, O, gate, up and down) with rank = 32 with a batch size of 1. We padded all sequences to a certain maximum sequence length to mimic long context finetuning workloads.

executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of Unsloth's capabilities for fine-tuning Gemma 4, summarizing its key advantages, and confirming its suitability for the proposed Next.js offline demo project.

unsloth_capabilities_overview.description: A detailed breakdown of Unsloth's features, including inference capabilities (running GGUF/safetensors), training optimizations (2x faster, 70% less VRAM), Reinforcement Learning (RL) support, and data processing with 'Data Recipes'.

free_tier_summary.description: Summary of the Free (open-source) tier, including its core features like support for major models and LoRA training.

pro_tier_summary.description: Summary of the Unsloth Pro tier, highlighting its speed and VRAM improvements for multi-GPU setups.

enterprise_tier_summary.description: Summary of the Unsloth Enterprise tier, detailing its benefits like multi-node support, accuracy boosts, and faster inference.

license_type.description: The name of the license for the open-source version, identified as 'Community License'.

studio_description.description: Description of Unsloth Studio as a no-code, offline web UI for training and running models.

studio_platforms.description: The operating systems Unsloth Studio runs on, such as Mac and Windows.

studio_key_features.description: Key features of Unsloth Studio, including the visual UI, 'Data Recipes' for dataset creation, and real-time observability.

open_source_description.description: Description of the open-source Unsloth library, which provides optimized kernels for faster training via code.

open_source_use_case.description: Typical use case for the open-source library, such as running in Google Colab, Kaggle, or local scripted environments.

supported_variants.description: A list of the supported Gemma 4 model variants, including E2B, E4B, 26B-A4B, and 31B.

supported_modalities.description: The modalities supported for Gemma 4 fine-tuning, such as vision, text, and audio.

performance_gains.description: Unsloth's claimed performance improvements for training Gemma 4, such as being ~1.5x faster with ~60% less VRAM.

key_bug_fixes.description: A summary of universal Gemma 4 training bugs that Unsloth has addressed, such as issues with gradient accumulation.

qlora_and_lora_workflows.description: An in-depth guide to using QLoRA and LoRA fine-tuning methods with Unsloth. This includes recommended hyperparameters (rank, alpha), targeting specific modules, and Unsloth's specific optimizations and bug fixes.

supported_dataset_formats.description: Information on the dataset formats compatible with Unsloth, such as conversational formats (ShareGPT, ChatML), instruction formats (Alpaca), and how to use the 'Data Recipes' feature to convert documents (PDF, CSV, JSON) into trainable datasets.

training_scripts_and_process.description: A guide on how to initiate and manage a training process using Unsloth, including example code snippets from Unsloth's Colab notebooks for setting up the model, tokenizer, dataset, and trainer for a fine-tuning job.

merged_model_export.description: Details on exporting a 'Merged Model', which combines the base model and LoRA adapter into a single 16-bit safetensors file.

lora_only_export.description: Details on exporting 'LoRA Only', which saves only the lightweight adapter weights and requires the original base model for inference.

gguf_export.description: Details on exporting to GGUF format, which is optimized for local CPU/GPU inference with tools like llama.cpp, Ollama, and Unsloth Studio.

project_fine_tuning_strategy.description: A recommended strategy for fine-tuning the Gemma 4 E2B-it model for the clinical urge surfing app. This includes using QLoRA for efficiency, suggestions for dataset creation focused on cognitive behavioral therapy and urge surfing techniques, and example data structures.

engine_option_transformers_js.description: Guidance on using the Transformers.js library with WebGPU to load and run the model and LoRA adapter directly in the browser.

engine_option_llama_cpp_wasm.description: Guidance on using a WebAssembly-based engine like llama.cpp.wasm to run an exported GGUF model file for efficient offline inference.

model_loading_workflow.description: An overview of the process for loading the chosen model format (GGUF or LoRA adapter) within the Next.js application for offline access.

ui_integration_summary.description: A summary of how to connect the inference engine to the Next.js UI components to create the interactive chat functionality for the app.

practical_recommendations_summary.description: A bulleted list of key, actionable recommendations for successfully completing the project, from model selection and training to deployment in the web app.
